name: VFX Forth
summary: MPE's commercial Forth, usually named when peak desktop performance matters. Native code generation, aggressive stack-traffic optimisation, inlining; vendor claims within ~25% of hand-written assembler on their published examples. Commercial closed-source.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
If the question is "which Forth is usually named when people want the *fastest desktop production compiler*?", the conservative answer is **VFX Forth**. MPE's own material claims it has long been the fastest Windows Forth, emphasises **native code generation**, **aggressive stack-traffic optimisation**, and **inlining**, and says the result gets within roughly 25% of hand-written assembler on their published examples.

This should be read as a **vendor claim**, not a neutral standards-body ranking, but it lines up with the reputation VFX has in practitioner discussions: if peak speed matters and commercial tooling is acceptable, VFX is usually in the first sentence.

sections.title: Position
sections.content:
Native code, a serious optimiser, and large-codebase credibility make VFX the usual top pick for "fastest serious desktop Forth," with the caveat that it is commercial/closed and much of the public benchmark evidence is uneven and vendor-hosted.

A 1.4M-line Forth codebase moved onto VFX Forth was reported (HN anecdote) to run ~10× faster than the earlier threaded-code build — anecdotal but consistent with the vendor's claims.
